title: Virgin Galactic Sees Astronaut Flight Resumption in 2026
text: Virgin Galactic announced plans to resume astronaut flights in 2026 with new pricing. The company's new Delta-class spacecraft will debut with a research mission in summer 2026, followed by private astronaut flights beginning in fall 2026. During an earnings call, CEO Michael Colglazier stated the company continues to expect its first research spaceflight will take place in summer of 2026, with private astronaut flights following in fall of 2026. The company currently has approximately 675 customers on its manifest and plans to resume accepting new flight reservations in Q1 2026. Pricing for new customers will exceed the previous $600,000 per seat cost, with tickets now priced at $750,000. Virgin Galactic will implement a wave-based sales strategy to manage customer onboarding and adjust pricing accordingly. The Delta-class spacecraft are designed to fly twice weekly—a significant increase from the VSS Unity's flight cadence. Additionally, Virgin Galactic is exploring a second spaceport location, potentially in southern Italy, and investigating multipurpose applications for its carrier aircraft.
